evidence.scientific_clarity: Violin+box+jitter overlay clearly shows distribution differences across all 4 conditions in correct order; significance brackets aid interpretation.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 in script; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmapped) DejaVuSans — text is not selectable/editable; DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default, not a publication font.
evidence.palette: Uses Seaborn-default 4-color categorical palette; red (#C44E52) and green (#55A868) appear together, problematic for ~8% red-green colorblind readers; no directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout applied, spines cleaned, xlim/ylim sensibly set, no overlapping labels or excessive whitespace visible from script logic.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and test details; no legend needed (x-axis labels suffice); missing a specific quantitative finding in caption would draw Reviewer 2 comment.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains Type 3 (bitmapped) font glyphs per pdffonts; script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is not truly vector/selectable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (28K), figure.png (180K), figure.svg (96K), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption describes plot elements and statistical test clearly with n=100 stated, but lacks a specific numerical finding (e.g., median viability reduction for Combo vs Control).

overall_strengths: Complete brief compliance: correct condition order, violin+box+jitter overlay, alpha=0.35, Mann-Whitney significance brackets all implemented.
All required output files present and non-trivial; layout is clean with removed top/right spines and tight_layout.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0): PDF embeds Type 3 bitmapped DejaVu Sans glyphs — text is not selectable or editable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
Red-green color pair (#C44E52 and #55A868) in the palette is problematic for red-green colorblind readers; caption lacks a specific quantitative finding.
